issue.title: mkdir/mkdirSync does not throw an error when directory already exists and recursive: true

fixDiff:
diff --git a/cli/tsc/dts/lib.deno.ns.d.ts b/cli/tsc/dts/lib.deno.ns.d.ts
index 49901a75bac7c9..d7a34edcbf9a17 100644
--- a/cli/tsc/dts/lib.deno.ns.d.ts
+++ b/cli/tsc/dts/lib.deno.ns.d.ts
@@ -2639,7 +2639,8 @@ declare namespace Deno {
    * await Deno.mkdir("restricted_access_dir", { mode: 0o700 });
    * ```
    *
-   * Defaults to throwing error if the directory already exists.
+   * Throws if the directory already exists, unless `recursive` is set to
+   * `true`.
    *
    * Requires `allow-write` permission.
    *
@@ -2659,7 +2660,8 @@ declare namespace Deno {
    * Deno.mkdirSync("restricted_access_dir", { mode: 0o700 });
    * ```
    *
-   * Defaults to throwing error if the directory already exists.
+   * Throws if the directory already exists, unless `recursive` is set to
+   * `true`.
    *
    * Requires `allow-write` permission.
    *
